phases.name: Auth Module Design
phases.taskPrompt:
You are building the Payments API. Your first task is to design and implement the authentication module.

Review the API specification, security review findings, and any prior team decisions. Then:

1. Confirm the token signing algorithm and justify your choice.
2. Design the JWT token structure (claims, expiry, refresh strategy).
3. Outline the token validation middleware.
4. Document your decisions clearly so a future developer can understand the reasoning.

Produce your design as an ADR (Architecture Decision Record) with Status, Context, Decision, and Consequences sections.

phases.name: Rate Limiting Implementation
phases.taskPrompt:
Continue building the Payments API. Your second task is to implement rate limiting.

Review the API specification and any prior decisions about the auth module. Then:

1. Choose and justify a rate limiting algorithm.
2. Design the rate limit response headers.
3. Handle the interaction between rate limiting and authentication (limits per user vs per API key).
4. Ensure your implementation is consistent with prior architectural decisions.

Produce your design as an ADR.

phases.name: Error Handling Refactor
phases.taskPrompt:
Continue building the Payments API. Your third task is to refactor error handling across the entire API.

Review ALL prior decisions from Phase 1 (authentication) and Phase 2 (rate limiting). Then:

1. Design a global error handling strategy that is consistent with ALL prior ADRs.
2. Ensure rate limit errors include the required headers.
3. Ensure authentication errors distinguish between 401 and 403.
4. Ensure all error responses use the agreed-upon format.
5. Verify that your design respects ALL critical constraints.

Produce:
- A refactored error handling design
- A checklist verifying consistency with all prior decisions
- A deployment readiness assessment

killVariants.label: Dirty kill
killVariants.description: Hard process kill after Phase 1 completion. No checkpoint, no summary. The replacement agent gets nothing from the conversation — only what MemoryCrux persisted.

killVariants.label: Clean kill
killVariants.description: Process dies after decision-context write succeeds but before a summary is composed. The replacement agent can find decisions in MemoryCrux but has no handoff narrative.

killVariants.label: Graceful handoff
killVariants.description: Agent checkpoints decision state and exits cleanly. The replacement agent gets a full checkpoint with decision summary.

decision_recall_at_phase_3.description: % of Phase 1 + 2 decisions retrievable at Phase 3 start

dirty_recovery_success.description: A1 recovery to ready-to-work without architectural drift

recovery_time.description: Time from new session start to usable project state

architecture_preservation.description: Human blind score for preserving prior decisions in Phase 3

token_efficiency_vs_c2.description: T2 or T3 prompt tokens relative to Flat-max
